mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding employment income and household spending capacity, which drives aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises real borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return is positive during triggered periods, or the signal's t-statistic against zero is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the nominal federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, deeply negative real interest rates signal monetary policy is behind the curve, forcing expectations of aggressive future tightening that depresses 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when the joint trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, unemployment below 4.5% signals extreme labor market tightness that accelerates wage costs and squeezes corporate profit margins, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered weeks during late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly increases in industrial production signal accelerating manufacturing momentum and rising capacity utilization, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered 20-day forward returns do not exceed non-triggered returns by at least 2% annualized over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts of more than 100 thousand units over three months signals accelerating construction activity and durable goods demand, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns through cyclical sector repricing.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumption collapses, leading to negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered episodes, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is not below -0.2.
